docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Travel Agent is a Trip Planning Assistant powered by Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology. The system utilizes the Yandex Geo Reviews 2023 dataset to provide location-aware recommendations for places, businesses, and services.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
• Data Pipeline: Prepares and analyzes the Yandex Geo Reviews dataset
  • Embedding Scripts: Implements comparsion of different embedding models for Russian language

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.13
  • DVC for data version control
  • AWS CLI configured for Yandex Cloud

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/miem-refugees/travel-agent.git
cd travel-agent

# Create a virtual environment with uv
uv venv

# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Install dev dependencies
uv sync --dev

# Install docs dependencies
uv sync --group docs

docs.title: Data Preparation
docs.text:
The project uses DVC to manage the data pipeline:

# Download and prepare the dataset
dvc repro prepare

# Analyze the dataset
dvc repro analyze

# Generate evaluation questions and datasets
dvc repro generate_moscow_rag_questions
dvc repro generate_saint_petersburg_rag_questions
dvc repro generate_norm_rubrics_questions

docs.title: Embedding Benchmark Results
docs.text: We've benchmarked several multilingual embedding models on our dataset to measure retrieval performance. The key metric we use is Mean Average Precision (MAP) at different k values.

docs.title: MAP@k Results
docs.text: model map@1 map@3 map@5 map@10 map@20 embedding_duration_sec benchmark_duration_sec total_duration_sec cointegrated/rubert-tiny2 0.19 0.21 0.19 0.18 0.16 16.09 11.24 27.32 DeepPavlov/rubert-base-cased-sentence 0.19 0.26 0.29 0.29 0.26 207.21 24.82 232.03 ai-forever/sbert_large_nlu_ru 0.23 0.26 0.27 0.27 0.24 674.55 32.81 707.37 ai-forever/sbert_large_mt_nlu_ru 0.3 0.36 0.35 0.34 0.31 675.73 33.34 709.07 sentence-transformers/distiluse-base-multilingual-cased-v1 0.3 0.37 0.4 0.36 0.35 123.22 17.4 140.62 sentence-transformers/distiluse-base-multilingual-cased-v2 0.23 0.31 0.3 0.3 0.28 124.14 17.38 141.52 sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 0.38 0.45 0.46 0.42 0.39 68.9 14.62 83.52 sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2 0.32 0.4 0.38 0.37 0.36 220.55 25.11 245.67 intfloat/multilingual-e5-large 0.45 0.5 0.52 0.49 0.46 857 33.62 890.62 intfloat/multilingual-e5-base 0.45 0.52 0.5 0.49 0.45 251.67 24.92 276.59 intfloat/multilingual-e5-small 0.45 0.54 0.52 0.49 0.45 80.22 14.37 94.59 ai-forever/ru-en-RoSBERTa 0.43 0.5 0.5 0.47 0.45 778.2 33.57 811.77 sergeyzh/BERTA 0.45 0.53 0.54 0.52 0.48 244.3 25.45 269.75 tfidf 0 0 0 0 0 0.31 1067.18 1067.5

docs.title: Visual Comparison
docs.text:
MAP@10 Performance Comparison
----------------------------------
paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2  ██████████████████████████████  0.326
paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2  ██████████████████████████████  0.324
distiluse-base-multilingual-cased-v2   █████████████████████████       0.248
distiluse-base-multilingual-cased-v1   ███████████████████             0.224
DeepPavlov/rubert-base-cased-sentence  ███████████████                 0.150

docs.title: Key Findings
docs.text:
• Best Overall Performance: The paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 model demonstrates the highest MAP scores across most k values, making it our top performer.
  • Size-Performance Balance: Despite having fewer parameters than mpnet-base, the MiniLM-L12 model shows slightly better performance, offering an excellent balance between model size and retrieval capability.
  • Russian Language Performance: Specialized Russian BERT model from DeepPavlov performs worse than the multilingual models, possibly due to the specific nature of our review dataset and query patterns.

Based on these results, we've chosen to prioritize paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 for our production implementation while exploring alternative and hybrid approaches to further improve performance.

docs.title: Retrieval Strategies
docs.text:
The project compares:

  • Dense Retrieval: Using vector embeddings to find semantically similar content
  • Sparse Retrieval: Using BM25 and SPLADE for keyword-based retrieval
  • Hybrid Retrieval: Combining dense and sparse approaches
  • Re-ranking: Using cross-encoders or LLM-based re-ranking to refine results

docs.title: 📚 Docs guide
docs.text: How to work with documentation

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
To work with the documentation, you'll need to install the documentation dependencies:

# Install documentation dependencies
uv sync --group docs

docs.title: Local Development Server
docs.text:
To preview the documentation locally while editing:

mkdocs serve

This will start a development server at http://127.0.0.1:8000 that automatically refreshes when you make changes to the documentation files.

docs.title: Adding New Pages
docs.text: To add a new page to the documentation create a Markdown file in the appropriate directory under docs/

docs.title: Adding Images and Assets
docs.text:
Store images and other assets in the docs/assets/ directory:

docs/assets/
├── images/            # For screenshots and diagrams
├── diagrams/          # For architectural diagrams
└── examples/          # For example files

Reference images in your Markdown using relative paths:

![Database Schema](../assets/images/database-schema.png)

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
To build a static version of the documentation:

mkdocs build

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
The documentation is automatically deployed when changes are pushed to the main branch. You can also manually deploy:

# Deploy to GitHub Pages
mkdocs gh-deploy
